title: cocculus pendulus
intro: Cocculus pendulus, also known as the rain tree or Asian arum, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its umbrella-shaped inflorescence. The plant's roots and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cocculus pendulus include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Cocculus pendulus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine practices.
